FOOD_HERO_NATURAL_LIGHT.rationale: Overhead window light, hero shot. f/5.6 keeps the entire dish sharp from front to back. Colour accuracy is critical — ISO 100 ensures maximum tonal fidelity.
FOOD_HERO_NATURAL_LIGHT.proTip: Position the dish at 45 degrees to a north-facing or diffused window. Use a white reflector opposite the window to fill shadows. Shoot tethered to check colour on a calibrated display.
FOOD_HERO_NATURAL_LIGHT.commonMistake: Shooting at f/2.8 for bokeh — individual food elements fall out of focus and the dish loses its story. f/5.6 keeps all elements readable.

FOOD_DARK_MOODY.rationale: Low-key atmospheric lighting from a single directional candle or rim light source. f/2.8 creates shallow DOF that isolates the hero element in deep shadow.
FOOD_DARK_MOODY.proTip: Use a single bare speedlight or LED panel at hard 90-degree side angle for maximum shadow drama. Black cards opposite the light increase the shadow depth further.
FOOD_DARK_MOODY.commonMistake: Over-lighting the shadow side — dark moody food photography depends on deep, unlit shadows. Resist the urge to fill the shadows with reflectors.

FOOD_OVERHEAD_FLAT_LAY.rationale: Flat lay requires the lens to be perfectly perpendicular overhead. f/8 keeps all elements in the composition sharp across the entire frame from edge to edge.
FOOD_OVERHEAD_FLAT_LAY.proTip: Use a tripod arm extended directly overhead with a bubble level. Shoot tethered so you can adjust composition without repositioning. Even, diffused window light or a large softbox overhead.
FOOD_OVERHEAD_FLAT_LAY.commonMistake: Shooting at a slight angle rather than perfectly overhead — even a 5-degree tilt creates perspective distortion that makes round plates look oval.

FOOD_STEAM_ACTION.rationale: Capture rising steam: fast shutter freezes individual steam tendrils rather than recording a blur. Backlight rims the steam to make it visible against dark backgrounds.
FOOD_STEAM_ACTION.proTip: Place a bare speedlight or continuous LED directly behind and slightly above the dish as a rim light. The steam only reads against a dark background — use black or deep-toned backdrop.
FOOD_STEAM_ACTION.commonMistake: Shooting in flat frontal light — steam is invisible without strong backlight. The setup requires deliberate directional rim lighting to capture steam effectively.

FOOD_RESTAURANT_AMBIENT.rationale: No-flash restaurant photography using ambient light only. Restaurant lighting is typically tungsten at EV 5-7. White balance must be manually set to the restaurant's colour temperature.
FOOD_RESTAURANT_AMBIENT.proTip: Set custom white balance from a white napkin or use Kelvin mode — auto white balance often goes wrong in mixed restaurant lighting. Find a table near a window for supplemental natural light.
FOOD_RESTAURANT_AMBIENT.commonMistake: Using the built-in flash — this blows out the dish, creates harsh shadows, and is disruptive to other diners. Ambient-only is both more flattering and more considerate.

FOOD_PRODUCT_STUDIO.rationale: Commercial catalogue product: maximum sharpness throughout, strobe-controlled lighting, fully repeatable results. f/11 maximises depth. Strobe synced at 1/160s.
FOOD_PRODUCT_STUDIO.proTip: Use a colour checker card on the first frame for accurate post-processing. Shoot tethered with live view to verify focus before committing. Document your lighting setup for future matching.
FOOD_PRODUCT_STUDIO.commonMistake: Inconsistent white balance between frames — impossible to match in post. Set a fixed Kelvin value and do not let AWB shift between shots.
